items.name: Louie's Wine Dive
items.story: Known for its wine list, brunch crowd, and polished neighborhood feel near Drake, Louie's Wine Dive announced it will pour its last glasses on May 31.
items.publicDescription: Known for its wine list, brunch crowd, and polished neighborhood feel near Drake, Louie's Wine Dive announced it will pour its last glasses on May 31.

items.name: Panka Peruvian
items.story: Panka Peruvian announced it would close in early April after nearly seven years on Ingersoll, where it built a loyal following for its warm hospitality and Peruvian cooking.
items.publicDescription: Known for elevated Peruvian dishes and a warm Ingersoll dining room, Panka closed in early April 2026 after nearly seven years.

items.name: False Nine Social Club
items.story: Mitzi's reopened as False Nine Social Club, shifting the space toward a matchday-inspired soccer bar and social club atmosphere.
items.publicDescription: The former Mitzi's space reopened as a soccer-forward social club with a matchday bar feel in West Des Moines.

items.name: Django
items.story: The French-inspired downtown restaurant Django permanently closed after an 18-year run, with March 14 serving as its final day.
items.publicDescription: The French-inspired downtown staple closed after 18 years, ending its run on March 14, 2026.

items.name: Pita Pit (Urbandale)
items.story: The last remaining Des Moines metro Pita Pit, at 4212 Merle Hay Road in Urbandale, closed in early 2026. The chain, known for made-to-order pita sandwiches with lean grilled meats, fresh vegetables, and a range of sauces, had once had multiple metro locations including Ingersoll, West Des Moines, and downtown on 4th Street.
items.publicDescription: The last Des Moines metro Pita Pit, a fast-casual spot on Merle Hay Road in Urbandale known for customizable made-to-order pita sandwiches, closed in early 2026.

items.name: Desi Fresh
items.story: Desi Fresh recently opened in Waukee as a combination Desi grocery and food outlet serving kabobs, tandoori, biryani, and other South Asian staples.
items.publicDescription: This Waukee newcomer blends a Desi grocery with a hot food counter serving kabobs, tandoori, and biryani.

items.name: Scoops by Beth
items.story: Scoops by Beth opened in Highland Park as part of the district's comeback, serving small-batch ice cream inspired by family recipes.
items.publicDescription: A small-batch Highland Park ice cream shop that opened as part of the neighborhood's ongoing revival.

items.name: Ocha Bubble Tea & Dessert Cafe
items.story: Ocha opened its downtown cafe and tea-room concept in the former Despensary space with expanded seating and desserts.
items.publicDescription: Ocha opened its downtown cafe and tea-room concept in the former Despensary space with expanded seating and desserts.

items.name: Bar Martinez
items.story: Bar Martinez opened in Highland Park as a polished cocktail lounge with a moody neighborhood-bar feel.
items.publicDescription: A polished Highland Park cocktail lounge that added a moodier late-night option to the neighborhood's dining strip.

items.name: Toyo Ramen & Japanese Street Food
items.story: Toyo Ramen opened at Prairie Trail with Hakata-style ramen, Japanese street snacks, and a story shaped by the owner's Tibetan roots and training in Japan.
items.publicDescription: This Prairie Trail ramen shop brought Hakata-style broth, Japanese street food, and a distinct Japanese-Tibetan story to Ankeny.

items.name: Heroics Sports Lounge
items.story: Iowa's first women's sports bar opened on Ingersoll, taking over the former Juniper Moon space and leaning into an inclusive fan-first identity.
items.publicDescription: Iowa's first women's sports bar opened on Ingersoll, taking over the former Juniper Moon space and leaning into an inclusive fan-first identity.

items.name: Highland Underground
items.story: Highland Underground opened as a retro cocktail lounge in the basement of the former Klein's Department Store building.
items.publicDescription: Highland Underground opened as a retro cocktail lounge in the basement of the former Klein's Department Store building.

items.name: Haiku
items.story: The longtime Drake-area Asian fusion restaurant closed after about 15 years, and later reporting cited the business as inactive.
items.publicDescription: The longtime Drake-area Asian fusion restaurant closed after about 15 years, and later reporting cited the business as inactive.

items.name: Bar Louie (Jordan Creek)
items.story: The Jordan Creek Town Center Bar Louie quietly closed in September 2025 as the chain continued restructuring after its March 2025 Chapter 11 bankruptcy. Despite originally not being slated for closure, the West Des Moines location -- open since May 2014 -- shut its doors over the weekend without explanation.
items.publicDescription: The Jordan Creek Town Center gastropub closed in September 2025 amid the chain's bankruptcy restructuring, ending an eleven-year run in West Des Moines.

items.name: Bubbies BBQ
items.story: Bubbies BBQ opened in Pleasant Hill with smoked meats, comfort sides, and a neighborhood pit-stop feel.
items.publicDescription: A Pleasant Hill barbecue spot built around smoked meats, comfort sides, and casual neighborhood energy.

items.name: Americana
items.story: After about 14 years downtown, Americana announced it would close after service on September 7.
items.publicDescription: After about 14 years downtown, Americana announced it would close after service on September 7.

items.name: Ritual Cafe
items.story: Ritual Cafe closed after a 20-year run, ending one of downtown's most recognizable coffee-and-community spaces.
items.publicDescription: Ritual Cafe closed after a 20-year run, ending one of downtown's most recognizable coffee-and-community spaces.

items.name: Tous les Jours
items.story: Tous les Jours opened in West Des Moines offering French-Asian pastries, cakes, and breads.

sources.label: Des Moines Register Essential Restaurants list

items.publicDescription: A West Des Moines bakery-cafe featuring a wide array of French-Asian pastries, cakes, and breads.

items.name: Masao
items.story: Masao opened in the former Miyabi 9 space, blending sushi and modern French cooking in one of the metro's most distinctive new dining rooms.
items.publicDescription: An East Village spot pairing sushi with modern French cooking in the former Miyabi 9 space.

items.name: Palms DSM
items.story: Palms DSM opened on Ingersoll with an upscale Afro-Caribbean approach, earning attention for dishes like braised oxtail and honey suya wings.
items.publicDescription: An Ingersoll restaurant with an upscale Afro-Caribbean menu, known for dishes like braised oxtail and honey suya wings.

items.name: Black Cat Ice Cream
items.story: Black Cat Ice Cream opened in Valley Junction with handcrafted small-batch ice cream in creative flavors.
items.publicDescription: A Valley Junction ice cream shop known for handcrafted small-batch flavors made on site.

items.name: Fong's Pizza (East Village)
items.story: Fong's reopened in the East Village at the former Peace Tree site, turning a relocation into a major new chapter for the brand.
items.publicDescription: Fong's reopened in the East Village at the former Peace Tree site, turning a relocation into a major new chapter for the brand.

items.name: 30Hop
items.story: 30Hop opened in Ankeny featuring 30 beers on tap paired with comfort food, global flavors, and a casual gastropub setting.
items.publicDescription: An Ankeny gastropub that pairs 30 beers on tap with comfort food and globally inspired plates.

items.name: Hawaiian Bros Island Grill
items.story: Hawaiian Bros Island Grill opened in Waukee with a no-frills menu of Hawaiian plate lunches featuring classics from the islands.
items.publicDescription: A Waukee outpost of the Hawaiian-style fast-casual chain, offering no-frills island plate lunches.

items.name: Lachele's Fine Foods and The Fitz
items.story: Cory Wendel's second Lachele's location opened in Highland Park, pairing the burger-focused Lachele's side with the more clubby cocktail-driven Fitz side.
items.publicDescription: This Highland Park opening paired a second Lachele's burger outpost with The Fitz, a clubbier bar-and-cocktail sidecar next door.

items.name: Pho Viet
items.story: Pho Viet opened on E.P. True Parkway with fresh Vietnamese specialties including pho, banh mi, and rice plates.
items.publicDescription: A West Des Moines Vietnamese restaurant on E.P. True Parkway serving pho and Vietnamese specialties.

items.name: Istanbul Grill Cafe & Bakery
items.story: Istanbul Grill Cafe & Bakery opened in Urbandale serving traditional and modern Turkish cuisine alongside an in-house bakery.
items.publicDescription: An Urbandale Turkish restaurant pairing traditional and modern Turkish dishes with an in-house bakery.

items.name: Sunset Lounge
items.story: Sunset Lounge opened in Norwalk with pub fare and a full bar.
items.publicDescription: A Norwalk pub serving standard bar fare and a full bar.

items.name: Blu Thai Food & Sushi
items.story: Blu Thai Food & Sushi served its final meals on New Year's Eve 2024, ending a run in its downtown location near the Pappajohn Sculpture Park. Des Moines City Hall plans to move into the building in 2026.
items.publicDescription: This Thai and sushi restaurant near the Pappajohn Sculpture Park closed on New Year's Eve 2024 to make way for Des Moines City Hall's move into the building.

items.name: St. Kilda Cafe & Bakery
items.story: After an eight-year run in the Harbach Lofts building south of MLK Parkway, St. Kilda Cafe & Bakery closed its flagship on December 22, 2024. Known for Australian-style coffee and healthy modern breakfast and lunch, the original location anchored owners Alexander and Whitney Hall's cafe empire. The Rook Room game lounge moved into the space in 2025.
items.publicDescription: The flagship of St. Kilda's Australian-style cafe trio closed in the Harbach Lofts building after an eight-year run, making way for The Rook Room game lounge.

items.name: Flora
items.story: Flora opened at the Greater Des Moines Botanical Garden in the space long occupied by Trellis.
items.publicDescription: Flora opened at the Greater Des Moines Botanical Garden in the space long occupied by Trellis.

items.name: Cowboy Mexican Bar & Grill
items.story: Cowboy Mexican opened in the former El Rodeo space near East Euclid and E. 14th, giving the corridor a new bar-and-grill tenant.
items.publicDescription: Cowboy Mexican opened in the former El Rodeo space near East Euclid and E. 14th, giving the corridor a new bar-and-grill tenant.

items.name: Early Bird East Village
items.story: Early Bird expanded again with an East Village location, bringing its brunch-heavy menu to Court Avenue.
items.publicDescription: Early Bird expanded again with an East Village location, bringing its brunch-heavy menu to Court Avenue.

items.name: Littleleaf Luncheonette
items.story: Mallory Holt and Ryan Nicholson opened Littleleaf Luncheonette in Waukee's Old Town after three years of planning, emphasizing sustainability and elevated comfort food with dishes like yellowfin tuna melts and seasonal smoothies paired with Parlor Coffee from Brooklyn.
items.publicDescription: A Waukee Old Town breakfast and lunch spot emphasizing sustainability, elevated comfort food, and Parlor Coffee from Brooklyn.

items.name: Saffron Indian Cuisine
items.story: Saffron Indian Cuisine opened serving a variety of authentic Indian recipes, from samosas to Indo-Chinese specialties.
items.publicDescription: An Indian restaurant pairing authentic classics like samosas with Indo-Chinese specialties.

items.name: Bussin' Eggroll and Rice Co.
items.story: The Equitable Building gained a new quick-lunch option when Bussin' Eggroll and Rice Co. opened on the ground floor.
items.publicDescription: The Equitable Building gained a new quick-lunch option when Bussin' Eggroll and Rice Co. opened on the ground floor.

items.name: Whiskey House
items.story: Whiskey House shut down after more than seven years as part of a cluster of Ankeny-area restaurant closures.
items.publicDescription: Whiskey House shut down after more than seven years as part of a cluster of Ankeny-area restaurant closures.

items.name: Tacos & Tequila La Cava
items.story: The Ankeny Mexican gastrobar closed with plans to relocate out of state, part of a difficult month for local restaurants there.
items.publicDescription: The Ankeny Mexican gastrobar closed with plans to relocate out of state, part of a difficult month for local restaurants there.

items.name: Luddy's Tavern
items.story: Luddy's Tavern had a short run before closing during the same June shakeout that also hit several other Ankeny spots.
items.publicDescription: Luddy's Tavern had a short run before closing during the same June shakeout that also hit several other Ankeny spots.

items.name: Hugo's Wood-Fired Kitchen
items.story: Lynn Pritchard's Hugo's opened in late May on the Meals on Wheels campus, adding wood-fired cooking and a full bar near Drake.
items.publicDescription: Lynn Pritchard's Hugo's opened in late May on the Meals on Wheels campus, adding wood-fired cooking and a full bar near Drake.

items.name: Wasabi Chi (Original)
items.story: Jay Wang's original Wasabi Chi near Merle Hay Mall closed after 13 years, citing aging building infrastructure. The location was the anchor for a wider Wasabi family of sushi restaurants now spread across the metro.
items.publicDescription: Jay Wang's original Merle Hay-area Wasabi Chi shuttered after 13 years, citing aging building infrastructure. Other Wasabi locations remain open across the metro.

items.name: Prime & Providence
items.story: Prime & Providence debuted as a new high-end steakhouse with a soft opening just before its formal mid-May launch.
items.publicDescription: Prime & Providence debuted as a new high-end steakhouse with a soft opening just before its formal mid-May launch.

items.name: Table 128
items.story: Chef Lynn Pritchard brought Table 128 back to life downtown at Gray's Landing, reviving the restaurant after its Clive run ended in 2021.
items.publicDescription: The modern American restaurant returned in a new downtown home at Gray's Landing after going dark in 2021.

items.name: Zavy Kitchen
items.story: The team behind Pho Real expanded into the East Village with Zavy Kitchen, adding a new Vietnamese option downtown.
items.publicDescription: The team behind Pho Real expanded into the East Village with Zavy Kitchen, adding a new Vietnamese option downtown.

items.name: The Handlebar
items.story: The Handlebar in Dallas Center reopened under a new chef in April 2024, rebooting a small-town bar-and-grill staple along the Raccoon River Valley Trail.
items.publicDescription: A Dallas Center bar-and-grill along the Raccoon River Valley Trail that reopened in April 2024 under new culinary leadership.

items.name: Club Car Ankeny
items.story: Club Car's Ankeny outpost, which had opened only the year before, permanently closed in June.
items.publicDescription: Club Car's Ankeny outpost, which had opened only the year before, permanently closed in June.

items.name: Gazali's
items.story: A metro Mediterranean staple since the 1980s, Gazali's closed its most recent Hickman Road location in Clive after citing insufficient traffic. The family-operated restaurant teased a future return in its announcement.
items.publicDescription: A longtime metro Mediterranean staple closed its Clive location after nearly four decades of serving the area under different storefronts, leaving open the possibility of a future return.

items.name: Ben's Burgers
items.story: Ben's Burgers, a small-footprint Ankeny burger spot, closed in late 2023, making way for a new tenant in the space.
items.publicDescription: An Ankeny burger spot that closed in late 2023.

items.name: Izumi Sushi & Hibachi
items.story: Izumi opened in Clive before the new year, adding another all-you-can-eat sushi and hibachi option to the western suburbs.
items.publicDescription: Izumi opened in Clive before the new year, adding another all-you-can-eat sushi and hibachi option to the western suburbs.

items.name: Boston Market
items.story: The Des Moines-area Boston Market closed as the national chain cratered from ~300 locations at the start of 2023 to fewer than 30 by early 2024, driven by legal actions, unpaid wages, and evictions.
items.publicDescription: Known for rotisserie chicken and comfort sides, the Des Moines Boston Market was one of hundreds nationally that shuttered as the chain collapsed in 2023.

items.name: Beer Can Alley
items.story: The country-music line-dancing bar closed before Thanksgiving 2023 after nearly 12 years in the Court Avenue District, just months after moving two blocks to a smaller space that added a pizza-and-wings menu.
items.publicDescription: The boot-scootin' country music bar closed before Thanksgiving 2023 after nearly 12 years in the Court Avenue District.

items.name: Kinship Brewing Co.
items.story: Kinship Brewing, the ambitious 6-acre dog-park-adjacent brewery and beer garden, faced foreclosure in October 2023 less than three years after opening. Lincoln Savings Bank filed to collect nearly $6 million in loans, and the in-house Barbarian fine-dining restaurant shuttered alongside it.
items.publicDescription: A sprawling Waukee brewery, beer garden, and dog park that faced foreclosure after less than three years. Chef-driven Barbarian, the fine-dining tasting menu restaurant inside the space, closed alongside it.

items.name: Barbarian
items.story: Chef Jacob Demars opened Barbarian inside Kinship Brewing in January 2023 as a Sunday-night prix fixe concept. When Kinship fell into foreclosure in October, Barbarian closed and Demars did not pursue a new home.
items.publicDescription: Chef Jacob Demars' Sunday-night prix fixe restaurant inside Kinship Brewing, which opened in January and closed alongside the brewery in October 2023.

items.name: Oak Park
items.story: Oak Park opened after heavy anticipation, anchoring a new fine-dining addition on the western half of Ingersoll.
items.publicDescription: Oak Park opened after heavy anticipation, anchoring a new fine-dining addition on the western half of Ingersoll.

items.name: Either/Or
items.story: The Lua Brewing team opened Either/Or in a converted auto-parts building with a more ambitious full-service menu.
items.publicDescription: The Lua Brewing team opened Either/Or in a converted auto-parts building with a more ambitious full-service menu.

items.name: Zora Bar & Rooftop
items.story: The $5 million Ingersoll Avenue rooftop bar closed in August 2023 amid a public nuisance lawsuit from the City of Des Moines, multiple shootings including one fatal, and hundreds of police calls. Owner Edwin Allen later filed a $10 million countersuit against the city. The building was foreclosed in October 2024.
items.publicDescription: A $5 million rooftop bar that closed in August 2023 after a string of violence, noise complaints, and a public nuisance lawsuit from the City of Des Moines.

items.name: Barrel House
items.story: The Quad Cities-based Barrel House chain closed its Des Moines Market District location after about a year in business and announced plans to find new Des Moines space, which has not materialized. RRACKSS Fine BBQ took over the space.
items.publicDescription: The Quad Cities-based burger, pizza, and taco chain closed its Market District location after about a year, making way for RRACKSS Fine BBQ.

items.name: Spaghetti Works
items.story: The longtime Court Avenue institution closed in March 2023 after failing to recover from pandemic-era losses.
items.publicDescription: The longtime Court Avenue institution closed in March 2023 after failing to recover from pandemic-era losses.

items.name: Maxie's Restaurant & Lounge
items.story: One of the metro's oldest restaurants closed after 56 years when its owner faced health issues and a sale failed to materialize in time.
items.publicDescription: One of the metro's oldest restaurants closed after 56 years when its owner faced health issues and a sale failed to materialize in time.

items.name: Gusto Pizza (Waukee)
items.story: The Gusto Pizza Waukee restaurant closed in February 2023, with the brand consolidating operations around its flagship and Ingersoll locations.
items.publicDescription: The Waukee location of the local Gusto Pizza chain closed in February 2023 as the brand consolidated its metro footprint.

items.name: Faustinos Taqueria
items.story: Faustinos opened in Franklin Plaza with Guerrero-rooted recipes and quickly became a local taco favorite.
items.publicDescription: Faustinos opened in Franklin Plaza with Guerrero-rooted recipes and quickly became a local taco favorite.

items.name: HomeGrown
items.story: HomeGrown opened in the Crescent Building as the restaurant's first Iowa location.
items.publicDescription: HomeGrown opened in the Crescent Building as the restaurant's first Iowa location.

items.name: Wahlburgers (Hy-Vee)
items.story: The Wahlburgers West Des Moines location, a franchise of the Mark Wahlberg-backed burger chain operated through a Hy-Vee partnership, closed in early 2023 as the grocer pulled back from standalone restaurants.
items.publicDescription: The West Des Moines Wahlburgers, a franchise of the Mark Wahlberg-backed burger chain operated through a Hy-Vee partnership, closed as the grocer pulled back from standalone restaurants.

items.name: Wahlburgers Jordan Creek
items.story: Hy-Vee pulled back from standalone Wahlburgers locations in early 2023, closing the original Jordan Creek outpost.
items.publicDescription: Hy-Vee pulled back from standalone Wahlburgers locations in early 2023, closing the original Jordan Creek outpost.

items.name: Cooper's Hawk Winery & Restaurant
items.story: Cooper's Hawk opened its first Iowa location in the former Granite City site on University Avenue.
items.publicDescription: Cooper's Hawk opened its first Iowa location in the former Granite City site on University Avenue.

items.name: 1717 Brewing Co.
items.story: 1717 Brewing shut its taproom after four years in business, becoming one of the more notable brewery losses of late 2022.
items.publicDescription: 1717 Brewing shut its taproom after four years in business, becoming one of the more notable brewery losses of late 2022.

items.name: Proof
items.story: Proof closed after service on Nov. 19, ending a highly regarded downtown tasting-menu run that many locals considered one of the city's best restaurants.
items.publicDescription: Proof closed after service on Nov. 19, ending a highly regarded downtown tasting-menu run that many locals considered one of the city's best restaurants.

items.name: Mistress Brewing Company
items.story: Mistress Brewing shuttered its Ankeny taproom in late 2022, adding to a string of Iowa brewery closures in that window.
items.publicDescription: A craft brewery and taproom in Ankeny that closed in late 2022.

items.name: Nina's Tacos
items.story: Nina's Tacos closed its south-side restaurant after multiple break-ins, shifting its focus back to the food-truck side of the business.
items.publicDescription: Nina's Tacos closed its south-side restaurant after multiple break-ins, shifting its focus back to the food-truck side of the business.

items.name: Gusto Pizza Ingersoll Relaunch
items.story: Gusto reopened in a larger Ingersoll location after a summer hiatus, turning a delayed pandemic-era expansion into one of the corridor's more meaningful 2022 relaunches.
items.publicDescription: Gusto reopened in a larger Ingersoll location after a summer hiatus, turning a delayed pandemic-era expansion into one of the corridor's more meaningful 2022 relaunches.

items.name: Home Touch Kitchen
items.story: Home Touch Kitchen opened on East Grand with Liberian and West African influences brought into an Afro-Soul format.
items.publicDescription: Home Touch Kitchen opened on East Grand with Liberian and West African influences brought into an Afro-Soul format.

items.name: Big Grove Brewery & Taproom
items.story: Big Grove opened its giant Sherman Hill taproom and restaurant in the former Crescent Chevrolet building, one of the metro's splashiest 2022 launches.
items.publicDescription: Big Grove opened its giant Sherman Hill taproom and restaurant in the former Crescent Chevrolet building, one of the metro's splashiest 2022 launches.

items.name: The Chicken Shack
items.story: The Las Vegas-based Chicken Shack entered the market with its first Iowa location on East Euclid.
items.publicDescription: The Las Vegas-based Chicken Shack entered the market with its first Iowa location on East Euclid.

items.name: The Continental
items.story: After two years away, The Continental returned in a new East Village space, reviving one of the neighborhood's signature cocktail-and-small-plates spots.
items.publicDescription: After two years away, The Continental returned in a new East Village space, reviving one of the neighborhood's signature cocktail-and-small-plates spots.

items.name: Rice Bowl
items.story: Rice Bowl permanently closed after serving Beaverdale for more than 45 years, ending a long Lee-family run.
items.publicDescription: Rice Bowl permanently closed after serving Beaverdale for more than 45 years, ending a long Lee-family run.

items.name: Casa Cancun Mexican Bar & Grill
items.story: Casa Cancun opened in the former Strudl Haus building on Indianola Avenue with Mexican and Venezuelan dishes.
items.publicDescription: Casa Cancun opened in the former Strudl Haus building on Indianola Avenue with Mexican and Venezuelan dishes.

items.name: Gusto Pizza West Des Moines
items.story: Gusto closed its West Des Moines location while consolidating around a newly reopened Des Moines flagship.
items.publicDescription: Gusto closed its West Des Moines location while consolidating around a newly reopened Des Moines flagship.

items.name: Tupelo Honey
items.story: Tupelo Honey finally opened its first Iowa restaurant after a long pandemic delay.
items.publicDescription: Tupelo Honey finally opened its first Iowa restaurant after a long pandemic delay.

items.name: 503 Cocktail Lab + Tasting Room
items.story: Chef Lynn Pritchard opened 503 as a drink lab and tasting room in the East Village, giving the area a clever small-footprint cocktail destination.
items.publicDescription: Chef Lynn Pritchard opened 503 as a drink lab and tasting room in the East Village, giving the area a clever small-footprint cocktail destination.

items.name: Mad Meatball
items.story: Mad Meatball returned to Des Moines at LINC at Gray's Station after closing its East Village location and delaying a comeback during the pandemic.
items.publicDescription: Mad Meatball returned to Des Moines at LINC at Gray's Station after closing its East Village location and delaying a comeback during the pandemic.

items.name: Dirt Burger!
items.story: Dirt Burger, a small independent burger spot, closed in early 2022 during the broader pandemic-era shake-out of metro restaurants.
items.publicDescription: A small independent metro burger spot that closed during the pandemic-era restaurant shake-out.

items.name: Ricochet
items.story: Ricochet finally opened its larger downtown game lounge after a pandemic-delayed move out of the East Village.
items.publicDescription: Ricochet finally opened its larger downtown game lounge after a pandemic-delayed move out of the East Village.

items.name: Hobnob Coffee and Wine Bar
items.story: Hobnob opened inside Hotel Fort Des Moines as a sister concept across from Proudfoot & Bird.
items.publicDescription: Hobnob opened inside Hotel Fort Des Moines as a sister concept across from Proudfoot & Bird.

items.name: Java Joe's
items.story: Java Joe's closed on 4th Street and announced a move to Ankeny, adding to the block's pandemic-era losses.
items.publicDescription: Java Joe's closed on 4th Street and announced a move to Ankeny, adding to the block's pandemic-era losses.

items.name: Proudfoot & Bird
items.story: Proudfoot & Bird opened with the revived Hotel Fort Des Moines project, restoring a major downtown dining room.
items.publicDescription: Proudfoot & Bird opened with the revived Hotel Fort Des Moines project, restoring a major downtown dining room.

items.name: Alleged Lee's
items.story: Alleged Lee's opened next to a reopened Quinton's East Village with a Prohibition-era Iowa theme and whiskey focus.
items.publicDescription: Alleged Lee's opened next to a reopened Quinton's East Village with a Prohibition-era Iowa theme and whiskey focus.

items.name: The New Northwestern
items.story: The New Northwestern opened in a restored historic building with an elegant small-plates, cocktails, and wine focus.
items.publicDescription: The New Northwestern opened in a restored historic building with an elegant small-plates, cocktails, and wine focus.

items.name: Dobre Tasting Room
items.story: Dobre arrived in the East Village in summer 2021 with one of Des Moines' first handcrafted seltzer-bar concepts, though it proved short-lived.
items.publicDescription: Dobre arrived in the East Village in summer 2021 with one of Des Moines' first handcrafted seltzer-bar concepts, though it proved short-lived.

items.name: TaKo
items.story: TaKo opened at Boomer's Bicycle Lounge with a Korean barbecue and Tex-Mex fusion menu.
items.publicDescription: TaKo opened at Boomer's Bicycle Lounge with a Korean barbecue and Tex-Mex fusion menu.

items.name: Good News, Darling
items.story: Good News, Darling opened behind Hello, Marjorie with an intimate, cocktail-forward format that quickly became a downtown favorite.
items.publicDescription: Good News, Darling opened behind Hello, Marjorie with an intimate, cocktail-forward format that quickly became a downtown favorite.

items.name: Riccelli's Italian Restaurant
items.story: Riccelli's is included as a major pandemic-era loss; later redevelopment and sale reporting confirmed the family had closed the longtime restaurant in 2020.
items.publicDescription: Riccelli's is included as a major pandemic-era loss; later redevelopment and sale reporting confirmed the family had closed the longtime restaurant in 2020.

items.name: George the Chili King
items.story: Known for chili, loose-meat sandwiches, malt shakes, and its old-school diner feel, George the Chili King remains one of the metro's most mourned closures.
items.publicDescription: Known for chili, loose-meat sandwiches, malt shakes, and its old-school diner feel, George the Chili King remains one of the metro's most mourned closures.

items.name: Chicago Dog
items.story: A Drake-area hot dog spot at 2314 University Ave that specialized in authentic Chicago-style dogs - all-beef with poppy seed buns, yellow mustard, onions, tomato, dill pickle, sport peppers, and celery salt. A separate downtown location on SW 9th also served Phillies and classic American fare.
items.publicDescription: A Drake-area hot dog joint known for authentic Chicago-style dogs with poppy seed buns, sport peppers, and celery salt.

items.name: Nomi's Asian Cuisine
items.story: Nomi's Asian Cuisine reopened in Altoona in September 2019 after an SUV drove into the building the previous month.
items.publicDescription: An Altoona Asian cuisine spot that reopened in September 2019 after recovering from a vehicle crashing into its building the previous month.

items.name: Reed's Hollow
items.story: An ambitious East Village restaurant that closed in late summer 2019.
items.publicDescription: An East Village New American restaurant that closed in late summer 2019.

items.name: Baru 66
items.story: A Spanish-style tapas and wine bar in the Western Gateway area that closed in summer 2019.
items.publicDescription: A tapas-and-wine bar in the Western Gateway area that closed in summer 2019.

items.name: Fighting Burrito
items.story: Fighting Burrito was a downtown fast-casual Mexican spot known for oversized burritos. Its downtown space later became the home of Blu Thai Food & Sushi.
items.publicDescription: A downtown fast-casual Mexican spot known for oversized burritos; its former space later became Blu Thai Food & Sushi.

items.name: Cowboy Chicken
items.story: The Dallas-based wood-fired rotisserie chicken chain closed its Ankeny location in late 2018 after a short run.
items.publicDescription: An Ankeny outpost of the Dallas-based wood-fired rotisserie chicken chain that closed after a short run.

items.name: Something Italian
items.story: A neighborhood Italian spot that closed in late 2017.
items.publicDescription: A neighborhood Italian spot that closed in late 2017.

items.name: Bear
items.story: Bear Restaurant in Ankeny closed in summer 2016.
items.publicDescription: An Ankeny New American restaurant that closed in summer 2016.

items.name: Overtime
items.story: Overtime reopened in Urbandale in March 2015 with a craft beer focus, replacing an earlier bar iteration in the same space.
items.publicDescription: An Urbandale sports bar that reopened in March 2015 with a craft beer focus.

items.name: Carlos O'Kelly's
items.story: A local outpost of the Tex-Mex chain best known for its Margarita Grilled Chicken and complimentary chips and salsa, which closed its metro location in 2014.
items.publicDescription: A Tex-Mex chain outpost remembered for its Margarita Grilled Chicken and chips-and-salsa service that closed in 2014.
